metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_3747
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The Cl--sensitive WNK1 phosphorylates the Cl-extruding K+-Cl- cotransporter KCC2.
Figure 1: WNK1 inhibition facilitates KCC2-dependent Cl- extrusion and causes a hyperpolarizing shift in GABA in immature, but not in mature, neurons.
Figure 2: In utero inhibition of WNK1 lowers neuronal [Cl-]i and causes a hyperpolarizing shift in GABA activity.
Figure 5: Inhibition of WNK1 activates KCC2 and decreases KCC2 inhibitory phosphorylation at Thr906 and Thr1007
Hence, WNK1 inhibits KCC2 to decrease neuronal Cl- extrusion capacity in immature neurons, thereby contributing to the maintenance of the depolarizing action of GABA

19/6/2018 Pim
- The data at the level of the synapse is not included in this paper. It is very well known (and also implied by the authors), however, that the GABAaRs studied in this paper are believed to represent postsynaptic receptors responsible for synaptic transmission (see Additional literature). I have approved of the annotation to SynGO despite the lack of direct evidence in synaptic context.. Experimental description: M&M: Hippocampi and cortices from 18-day-old rat embryos
